Job Title: Product Manager - Finance Systems & Data Platforms
Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Product Manager to lead the strategy and delivery of a portfolio of finance systems and data platforms across the Banking & Financial Services environment.

In this role, you will define the product vision, roadmap, and priorities for key platforms, including general ledger, accounts receivable, reconciliations, budgeting and forecasting, and finance analytics. You will work closely with finance, technology, and architecture teams to translate complex accounting and tax requirements into clear problem statements, prioritised backlogs, and scalable system solutions.

You will also help shape how financial data is structured, governed, and integrated across platforms, while identifying opportunities to leverage AI and automation to modernise finance capabilities.

Key Requirements

8+ years’ experience in product management or product ownership, delivering data-rich enterprise platforms
Strong understanding of finance systems, accounting processes, and data flows
Experience working with general ledger and EPM platforms (e.g. Oracle, Anaplan, TM1)
Proven ability to translate business requirements into prioritised backlogs and deliver through agile teams (Jira)
Knowledge of data governance, lineage, and integration patterns
Strong stakeholder management across finance and technology teams
This role offers the opportunity to play a key part in modernising the finance technology ecosystem and enabling better data, reporting, and decision-making across the organisation.

The Company

My client is a highly successful and well-known logistics company who are that has experienced double digit growth over the last 3 years. They boast an impressive client base across a range of sectors, employ high performing individuals and foster a culture of inclusivity and continued chances for career development.

The Role

An exciting opportunity has arisen within the Group FP&A team. As the FP&A Analyst, you will report into the Group FP&A Manager and have the following responsibilities:

Preparation and delivery of Group wide budgets and forecasts
Consolidated monthly management reporting
Variance analysis and commentaries and development of KPI tracking across the Business
Develop and maintain accurate financial models to support key business units and major capex projects
Business partner with key divisions in achieving budgeted targets
Process improvements
Mentor and develop a junior analyst
Ad hoc project involvement
What is Required?

Whilst CA/CPA/CIMA qualified is a preference it is more about your skill set (you''l need to love financial modelling and analysis!)
Exposure to logistics and mining services will be seen as advantageous but not essential
Experience managing multiple deadlines and exemplary organisation skills
Advanced Excel skills and experience in using Business Intelligence tools such as PowerBI
You will be curious and a proactive team player
Experience working with non-finance stakeholders and being able to articulate key finance concepts
Why You'll Want to Join
Work for a high‑profile industry leader with national operations.
Play a key role in modernising and uplifting the finance function.
Flexibility with 1 day working from home a week
Lead a capable team, influence decision‑making and drive meaningful change.
Enjoy career development pathways in a growing and evolving business.
Be part of a supportive, collaborative culture that values accountability, integrity and continuous improvement.
Ready to Step Up? If you're looking to step up into your first role developing junior staff and a role where you can make a real impact, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now to Briony Gibson at bgibson@fourquarters.com.au to join a forward‑thinking organisation where your expertise will shape smarter financial outcomes and better operational performance.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Wealth Management Platform
Ad Text:
This opportunity suits an experienced Senior Business Analyst who understands the wealth management platform environment and enjoys shaping delivery outcomes across complex projects. This role sits within a leading Australian wealth management technology provider that is delivering a significant pipeline of transformation work.

You will lead analysis across discovery, planning and delivery. You will work closely with product specialists, operations teams, technology groups and external partners to define business needs and support timely project execution. You will run workshops, capture requirements, document artefacts and support change activities including impact assessments and readiness planning. You will also contribute to continuous improvement across project services and tools.

12 month fixed term contract (potential to extend)
Sydney - hybrid working
To be successful you will bring:

At least 3 years as a Business Analyst in wealth management
Must have strong wealth management platform experience 
Strong analytical and documentation skills
Confident stakeholder engagement and facilitation capability
Experience with large-scale transformation work
Proficiency in MS Office, Jira, Confluence 
A proactive and structured approach to problem solving
This role offers variety, responsibility and direct access to senior stakeholders in a modern platform environment. If this role is of interest, please apply.

Suited to a chartered accountant with retail or consumer goods experience
Dynamic culture with an excellent modern head office based in the city fringe 
Up to $145,000 package | Exciting perks | Flexible working arrangements

The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to step into a fast growing fun ecommerce environment as a Senior Financial Analyst where your primary focus is making finance better, not just keeping the lights on. You will work directly with the Head of Finance to assist with redesigning processes, automate reporting, managing commercial reporting and support the businesses next phase of growth. 

The Challenge

Reporting to the Head of Finance, you will be responsible for but not limited to the following: 

Lead forecasting and budgeting process 
Migrate cash flow reporting into a structured, systematic solution, enabling variance analysis against forecast and reducing manual tracking.
Provide full P&L financial support on commercial arrangements.
Providing analysis of commercial information and advising key operational staff to drive performance
Providing analytical support to the strategic planning process;
Proactively identify further process and systems improvement opportunities to drive efficiency and scalability as the business grows.
Provide business partnering, financial modelling and business case support on projects
The Expertise

Please consider expressing interest in this position if you possess the following:

You are CA/CPA qualified with 4-7 years of total experience. Ideally, you have consumer goods/retail/FMCG experience in an analytically focused role
Strong budgeting and forecasting skills
Strong process improvement and partnering experience with excellent Excel skills
It's likely that you will have begun your career within the Big 4 or mid-tier before spending 1-3 years working in industry looking to make your second move. 
The Next Step

If this position will enhance your career and you would like further details on the client, the role, remuneration or a copy of the position description, please call Thomas Burt for a confidential discussion on 0427 465 934.
Or alternatively please apply per the link below.

Research Analyst Internship - Full Time Position (Monday-Friday)

About KOSEC:

KOSEC – Kodari Securities, is one of Australia’s most accomplished financial institutions, has established a legacy of expertise, strategic insights, and ground-breaking research. At the heart of KOSEC lies our mission: to empower investors with unparalleled knowledge, state-of-the-art tools, and abundant resources. Committed to optimal avenues of capital preservation and wealth cultivation, KOSEC serves as a beacon for ultra-high net worth investors, corporations, trusts, SMSFs, family offices, and charities.

Program Overview:

A rare 3-month full time internship opportunity awaits at KOSEC, tailored for individuals keen to immerse themselves in the world of equity markets. 

Why KOSEC Internship?

Comprehensive Training: Delve deep into sectors and companies, master the art of daily technical analysis, and gain a comprehensive perspective of the equity market's dynamism.

State-of-the-Art Tools: Acquaint yourself with Phantom X, a ground-breaking in-house software, and redefine your analytical skill set.

Guided by the Elite: Receive guidance from industry pioneers, notably KOSEC’s visionary CEO, Michael Kodari.

Core Values at KOSEC:

Excellence: Every endeavour at KOSEC aims to surpass client expectations, setting new benchmarks in products and services.

Client-Focused Approach: Each move is calibrated towards client satisfaction, feedback, and a vision for continuous improvement.

Integrity & Accountability: At KOSEC, promises transition into unwavering commitments, and responsibilities are shouldered to bring about tangible results.

Innovation: Disruption is in the institution's DNA, constantly ushering in pioneering ideas that raise industry standards.

Transparency: Ensuring that every decision is made with utmost clarity, stakeholders are always in the loop and empowered.

Respect: Central to KOSEC's philosophy, it forges a nourishing and collaborative work environment.

Introducing Your Mentor, Michael Kodari:

Michael Kodari is widely recognized as a top-performing investor, philanthropist, and leading expert in financial markets on an international scale.

Globally acclaimed as an investment luminary, Michael consistently delivers exceptional performance, setting himself apart on the world stage. Beyond his philanthropic endeavours, he is acknowledged as one of the foremost authorities in the financial markets, earning the distinction of being hailed by CNBC Asia as 'the brightest 21st-century entrepreneur in wealth management.'

With a solid foundation in funds management and stockbroking, Michael's journey is characterised by collaborations with some of the industry's most successful minds and consultations with leading financial institutions. A trailblazer from the outset, he became the youngest expert panellist on the Sky Business Channel at a mere 25 years old, captivating audiences and achieving record-high ratings. Over three transformative years, Michael solidified his reputation as an unwavering force in the field.

His influence extends beyond the financial sector to television screens and print media, where he assumes the role of 'Money Expert' on Channel 7's weekly Sunday program 'Elevator Pitch' and contributes insights to key financial publications. With a magnetic presence, he engages in interviews across diverse media platforms and spearheads enlightening global conferences.

Michael's impact reaches the highest echelons, earning endorsements from governments worldwide. His distinguished participation in the landmark event 'Inside China’s Future,' hosted in Australia and honoured by the Chinese government, underscores his prowess in fund management. Standing alongside George Soros, he was the sole financier invited to offer invaluable insights on Chinese government asset allocation offshore, a testament to his extraordinary expertise.

In 2019, Michael's reputation was further amplified as he took centre stage as an esteemed expert panellist at Parliament House during the Australia-China Innovation & Entrepreneurship Forum, meticulously orchestrated by AUSTRAC in collaboration with the Chinese government. His profound insights and expertise significantly contributed to discussions, shedding light on the dynamic landscape of innovation and entrepreneurship between the two nations. This participation solidified his role as a trusted authority in the field of investment and collaboration on an international scale.

Your Future with KOSEC:

· Engage with diverse clientele, including individual investors, family offices, trusts, SMSFs, corporations, and charities.

· Address real-world financial challenges, providing a robust practical learning environment.

· Network with industry leaders and benefit from unparalleled mentorship opportunities.

· Gain exposure to transformative financial methodologies and practices.

Alinta Energy is one of Australia’s biggest energy retailers, generators and developers with over 1.1 million customers. Australia’s energy system is undergoing the biggest transformation in its history and we’re right there with it. We’re investing in and developing large-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

We understand that flexibility matters when considering your next role. Let us know your requirements, and we’ll carefully consider how we might accommodate them.

Ready to make a real impact?

Step into a key role within Alinta Energy’s Group FP&A function, where you’ll help shape group level financial insight and executive decision making. Reporting to the FP&A Manager, this role operates at a group wide level, consolidating financial performance, supporting forecasting and budgeting, and producing executive ready reporting. The position is offered as a maximum term contract for 18 months (parental leave cover) and plays an important role in maintaining continuity, accuracy, and confidence in financial reporting within a complex and fast paced environment. This is a highly visible role that combines strong financial governance, analytical depth, and clear financial storytelling.

Why you’ll love working here

At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia — and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.

There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.

What you'll do

Deliver group level performance reporting (month-end, rolling forecast, board/executive packs) with clear variance analysis, risks, opportunities, and strong cash flow insights.
Support budgeting and forecasting timetables, partnering with finance teams and business partners to build accurate, forward-looking views.
Enhance financial models and Power BI dashboards, driving automation and consistent reporting in partnership with systems teams.
Respond to ad hoc analysis and executive queries, including owners and government.
Identify and lead process improvements across reporting and analytics to reduce manual effort and lift quality and speed.

Your experience

Proven experience as a Senior Analyst (FP&A/financial analysis) in a large or complex environment, producing executive-level insights.
Strong accounting foundation with a tertiary finance/accounting degree and CA/CPA (or equivalent).
Recent hands on with Oracle EPM (FCCS/PBCS), advanced Excel, and Power BI.
Exceptional written and verbal communication; able to translate complex data into clear narratives for senior stakeholders.
Proactive, analytical, and resilient, with the confidence to challenge assumptions, influence stakeholders, and meet hard deadlines.

Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.

Like what you’re seeing?

If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and discover better together with Alinta Energy.

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.

Join our team as a Product Manager within our Consumer Product team. This role can be based in Brisbane or Melbourne. You will be accountable for the strategy and execution of our collections and recoveries platform, helping Australia and New Zealand’s largest lenders and businesses optimise collections performance and make smarter, fairer decisions across the end‑to‑end credit lifecycle.


You will be a key leader in defining the product vision for our collections and recoveries platform, owning the roadmap, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver innovative collections and recoveries solutions that drive measurable outcomes for our clients and growth for Equifax. We are modernising our collections platforms and processes, so you’ll be balancing strategic change with hands-on delivery in an evolving technology environment. 


What you’ll do

Own the Market Strategy: Lead the overall Market Understanding for your platform. This includes driving market analysis, industry research, and deep-dive competitive evaluations to unlock and articulate high-potential growth opportunities.

Define the Future Product: Define the product's next chapter. You will assess and own strategic partnerships, define clear product requirements based on market needs, and decide on build, buy, or partner approaches. You'll also facilitate the development of the commercial model and packaging.

Drive Product Vision and Roadmap: Translate the product vision into an actionable strategy. This means developing strong business cases, defining the product roadmap across the Product Delivery Value Stream, and meticulously tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) to ensure objective attainment.

Deliver and Launch with Impact: Oversee the end-to-end product development lifecycle. You will write precise requirements, prepare for seamless product releases, and actively contribute to the prioritization of opportunities and critical product trade-offs.

Accelerate Growth & Portfolio Success: Ensure the product’s commercial success. You will track and monitor performance against revenue and margin targets, manage the portfolio to align new capabilities with customer demand, and use data analysis to drive key lifecycle decisions (enhancements and sunsetting).


What you’ll need 

Proven Product Leadership: 5-7 years of experience in product management or business analysis within a product-led organization, ideally in Technology, Financial Services, or Data & Analytics.

Required hands-on experience utilizing Agile methodologies and/or formal project management frameworks.

Demonstrated fundamental experience in writing detailed product requirements—you must be able to articulate the 'what,' 'who,' 'why,' and 'how' of the solution, focusing on business value (ROI).

Proven ability to organize, influence, and coordinate efforts effectively within a complex, highly matrixed corporate environment.

Strong preference for candidates with a demonstrated understanding of delivering cloud-based products and services.

Experience with core product tools is preferable (e.g., Aha, JIRA, Figma).

A plus if you have experience applying Design Thinking principles for innovation, including the use of personas, empathy mapping, prototyping, and iteration models to solve real-world needs.

What could set you apart

Experience in the Collections and Recoveries sector.

We believe that when we’re at our best, we can do our best. Equifax offers policies and programs that support our employees’ financial and personal wellbeing with additional leave days, hybrid working environment, employee assistance program and organizational growth potential through our online learning platform with guided career tracks.

Are you ready to power your possible? Apply today, and get started on a path toward an exciting new career at Equifax, where you can make a difference!

Who is Equifax? 

At Equifax, we believe knowledge drives progress. As a global data, analytics and technology company, we play an essential role in the global economy by helping employers, employees, financial institutions and government agencies make critical decisions with greater confidence. ​​​​​​​ 

We work to help create seamless and positive experiences during life’s pivotal moments: applying for jobs or a mortgage, financing an education or buying a car. Our impact is real and to accomplish our goals we focus on nurturing our people for career advancement and their learning and development, supporting our next generation of leaders, maintaining an inclusive and diverse work environment, and regularly engaging and recognizing our employees. Regardless of location or role, the individual and collective work of our employees makes a difference and we are looking for talented team players to join us as we help people live their financial best. 

Equifax is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics.

Company Description

Let’s create a more sociable future together

At Endeavour, we’re totally into what we do. With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, we love to bring people together. Together we share our passion for our products and industry; it’s what inspires us to dream big, and continue to create new experiences for our customers and teams across Australia. Job Description
This is just the start, so dream big 
Be part of a high-visibility program

At Endeavour Group, we are embarking on a landmark transformation through our One Endeavour Program. We are seeking a Finance Transformation Partner to serve as a key architect in the design, build, and delivery of our future-state finance framework. Working alongside our technology partners (OneStream and Taysols), you will help replace legacy constraints with high-performance platforms for Financial Close, Consolidation, Reconciliation, and ESG reporting.

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

As a Finance Transformation Partner, you aren’t just implementing software; you are re-engineering the way we work. You will bridge the gap between business requirements and technical execution, ensuring our new solutions deliver faster month-ends, bulletproof governance, and automated workflows.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and configure automated workflows for Financial Close and ESG solutions, shifting the focus from manual intervention to value-add analysis.
Partner with the Finance Transformation Manager and external consultants to ensure the OneStream implementation hits all milestones—from data migration to go-live.

About The Role

At HammondCare, data and information are critical to delivering safe, high quality care, maintaining trust with the people we serve, and enabling responsible innovation.

The Data Governance Lead is accountable for leading the design, implementation, and ongoing operation of HammondCare's enterprise data governance framework, ensuring data and information assets are trusted, high‑quality, secure, compliant, and ethically managed across the organisation.



This role translates HammondCare's enterprise data and information management strategy into practical, fit‑for‑purpose governance standards, processes, and controls. Working closely with clinical, operational, technology, analytics, privacy, and risk stakeholders, the Data Governance Lead embeds governance into day to day operations, decision making, and change initiatives enabling teams to use data confidently while meeting regulatory, privacy, and ethical obligations.



This is a hands on leadership role focused on stewardship, clarity, and adoption, ensuring governance enables care, insight, and innovation rather than slowing it down.



This is a permanent full-time role working at St Leonards with hybrid opportunities.

Job Description


Where and how you can work

This role is based in our flagship campus in Sydney. But you have choice in where and how you work. That means if you want to do your thing in the office (if you're near one), at home or a bit of both, it's up to you!

About the role

As the Chief of Staff (known internally as Leadership Operations), you’ll act as a force multiplier to the lead of one of Canva’s largest (over 500+) and most impactful orgs - Content & Discovery (C&D).

The 500+ C&D team plays a key role in Canva’s mission by building the world’s most useful, relevant, diverse, and inspiring content library, and helping users discover the best content for their job to be done.

In this role, you’ll extend the reach, capacity and impact of the C&D Lead, driving clarity, prioritisation, and alignment across strategic business priorities that span product, communications, operations, and leadership engagement.

You’ll play a critical role in enabling the success of the C&D Lead, their leadership team, and the broader C&D organisation by providing strategic direction, building the right spaces for effective collaboration, and proactively solving business challenges that unlock scale and impact.

You’ll also lead C&D’s operations team, made up of Operations Business Partners and Admin Business Partners (Executive Assistant). While the Admin BPs focus on unlocking capacity for leaders through world-class support, the Ops BPs partner with teams to deliver on core operational cadences, strategic planning, and goal execution. Together, you’ll help C&D deliver on its crazy big goals.

If you thrive in ambiguity, love juggling multiple competing priorities, and enjoy connecting the dots across people and priorities, this role is for you.

What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales, change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. We move fast here at Canva, and this role will certainly keep you on your toes.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

Strategic partner to C&D Lead: Act as a trusted thought partner across product strategy and long term direction. Help define and sharpen major bets across content supply, discovery, ranking, and user experience. Bring structured thinking to trade-offs, sequencing, and investment decisions.

Represent the Lead: Step into product and cross-functional forums on behalf of the C&D Lead. Ensure discussions are grounded in user impact, clear hypotheses, and defined success metrics.

Proactively drive projects forward: Driving key projects and initiatives that are critical to C&D. This will include cross-cutting project management with stakeholders that sit in many different parts of Canva. 

Drive planning and operational rhythm: Support key business cadences like QBRs and goal planning. Help build context and alignment across a complex org of 45+ team leads, including document creation and facilitation.

Planning and operations: Coach the Operations team to strengthen roadmap visibility, dependency management, and performance tracking. Ensure the org has a clear view of what is driving user value and where course correction is required.

Triage and prioritisation: Act as the first point of contact for inbound requests, founder asks, new initiatives, and escalations. Evaluate opportunities through a product and impact lens, and ensure focus remains on the highest leverage work.

Strong people connector: Build strong and trusted relationships with key stakeholders across the business to ensure visibility, and alignment between C&D and the business.

Own the day to day: In partnership with the Admin BP, help ensure the Lead’s time is spent where it matters most—triaging meetings, shaping agendas, and creating space for high-impact work.

Zoom in and out: Move seamlessly between long term product vision and operational detail. Use data, user insights, and performance trends to inform decisions while keeping sight of Canva’s broader growth strategy.

You're probably a match if you have

8 + years of experience in strategy, product, general management, or business leadership roles. Experience in SaaS or product led organisations is strongly preferred. You have operated close to product and growth decisions, not just supported them. Backgrounds in consulting, investment banking, private equity, or similar analytical environments are also valued, particularly where you have moved beyond analysis into driving decisions and outcomes.

Strong commercial instinct and product judgment. You naturally connect user impact, strategy, and business outcomes, and can articulate clear trade offs between competing bets.

High ownership mindset. You act like a GM, think long term, and focus on impact over activity.

Proven operational expertise, you have built or run planning cadences, OKRs, or execution frameworks across complex, multi team environments, and improved them over time.

Excellent communication and stakeholder skills, you are comfortable influencing senior leaders, challenging thinking constructively, and driving alignment across Product, Growth, Data, and Operations.

An ability to operate in ambiguity with a strong bias for action and instinct for when to escalate, delegate, or drive things yourself, with the ability to move complex and critical initiatives forward, often navigating the new and unknown. 

Great visual communication and design skills, especially when simplifying complex ideas(Canva’s made that bit easy!).

Clear and compelling communicator. You can simplify complex ideas into crisp narratives and decision briefs for senior audiences.

Highly efficient and proactive, able to juggle priorities, manage competing demands, and stay calm under pressure.

Data literacy and technical fluency with confidence navigating metrics, dashboards, and operational tools.

Operational leadership experience having led or coached high-performing teams in planning, strategy, or operations.

Self-motivation and a growth mindset, you move quickly, learn fast, and bring structure to complexity without waiting for perfect information.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Who is Plenti? 

Plenti is a fintech lender, providing faster, fairer loans by leveraging its smart technology. Plenti is a dynamic and innovative business that is growing strongly. By continuing to deliver better customer experiences, Plenti is taking market share from incumbent players in the personal lending, renewable energy, and automotive finance markets.

We are a fast moving and ambitious business that seeks to recruit smart and capable people, who can take ownership of their role to help the business thrive. With over 250 people based in Australia, Plenti is of a size where everyone can make a difference in their role and help us realise our very big ambitions as a team, as we go about building Australia’s best lender.

Plenti is a founder led business that launched in 2014, listed on the ASX since 2020 with annual revenue of over $250 million and a loan portfolio of over $2.5 billion.


About the Role:
As a Senior Platform Product Manager, you will co-lead our Finance & Servicing platform domain, driving the roadmap and delivery of the systems that underpin financial operations, regulatory reporting and servicing. This role offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of product, engineering and finance, solving complex platform challenges at scale. 

Key Responsibilities: 

Define and maintain the Domain Charter, including scope, interfaces and contracts, KPIs, SLAs, compliance controls, data ownership and quality standards

Lead end-to-end ownership of outcomes, including long-term strategic roadmap, delivery, QA, release planning, incident leadership and continuous improvement

Manage cross-domain dependencies, partnering with Origination, Credit and other teams to sequence work, negotiate trade-offs, coordinate cutovers and ensure backward-compatible interface versioning

Lead strategic platform initiatives such as ledger capability rollouts, reporting services or platform rebuilds, from business case through to delivery

Oversee regulatory and partner reporting, ensuring timeliness, accuracy and robust error handling across reporting obligations.

Support warehouse and servicing operations, including BAU reporting, trustee or treasury deliverables, and platform readiness for warehouse lifecycle activities

Maintain and communicate the platform roadmap, ensuring stakeholder alignment while identifying bottlenecks, technical debt and redesign opportunities

Partner closely with architects and engineering leadership to shape a cohesive platform strategy and delivery approach

Provide clear stakeholder and executive communication on progress, risks and key decisions, and facilitate governance and design forums

Act as a leader within the product and engineering teams by driving continuous improvement in product management practices and mentoring junior team members

About You: 

7+ years of product management experience, including significant experience in platform or core financial systems

Experience in two or more of the following areas: core banking or ledger platforms, payments and reconciliations, securitisation or warehouse platforms, or financial/regulatory reporting

Proven experience owning finance or post-origination platform domains end-to-end, including incident leadership and control outcomes

Experience leading major platform rebuilds or foundational service improvements across multiple teams

Strong systems thinking and technical fluency, with the ability to work closely with engineers on platform architecture, data flows, APIs and financial controls

Excellent stakeholder management, structured problem solving and executive communication skills

Experience with Australian lending and reporting frameworks (CCR or ATO) is desirable but not essential

Exposure to payment rails, securitisation, servicer reporting or data governance and control frameworks is desirable but not essential 

What's life like at Plenti? 

Working at Plenti means joining a team that is high-performing, diverse, customer-focused and collaborative. It’s a place where everyone can “make it happen” and share in the results and success. Plenti is committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Job Title: Principal Analyst – Data & Analytics Consulting | $200k+ Potential
Ad Text:

Location: Perth WA
Company: Gemcutter Analytics

Gemcutter Analytics is a fast-growing data analytics consultancy helping mining, construction and industrial businesses make better decisions through Power BI dashboards and business analytics.

We are looking for a Principal Analyst to lead client engagements and help build the next stage of our firm.

This is not a traditional corporate analytics job.

It is designed for someone who wants ownership, responsibility and significant upside.

The role offers a competitive base salary plus a high profit share structure, giving high performers the ability to earn $200k+ per year while building their own portfolio of clients and delivery team.

If you are looking for a comfortable internal analytics role with minimal commercial responsibility, this probably isn’t the role for you.

But if you are ambitious, commercially minded, and want the opportunity to build something meaningful, this could be a career-defining opportunity.







The Opportunity

As a Principal Analyst, you will manage a portfolio of client engagements and lead the delivery of analytics solutions that drive real business impact.

Our work focuses primarily on building dashboards and data models that help executive teams understand the drivers of their business.

You will act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, while leading internal delivery teams to ensure projects are delivered to a high standard.

You will have the opportunity to build and lead your own team within Gemcutter Analytics, creating a “business within a business", where you'll be responsible for your own PnL.







What You'll Be Responsible For

Managing Client Relationships

Acting as the primary relationship manager for key client accounts

Understanding each client’s business drivers and strategic goals

Ensuring clients receive exceptional service and tangible value from analytics

Leading Analytics Projects

Contributing to the commercial growth of the firm

Building and Leading a Team

Supporting hiring as the team grows

Developing scalable delivery processes

Building a high-performing analytics team around you







Who This Role Is For

This role suits someone who is ready for the next level but feels limited in a traditional corporate environment.

The Opportunity
  
Elysium Digital is expanding its AI consulting practice, and we’re looking for an experienced and forward-thinking consultant to help shape and lead our work in this exciting and fast-evolving space. As an AI Strategy & Enablement Consultant, you’ll work with public and private sector clients to design and deliver impactful strategies, frameworks and pilots that enable the responsible and effective adoption of artificial intelligence.
  
This is a hands-on consulting role with strong growth potential. You’ll support client engagements across AI readiness, prototyping, governance, enablement and architecture, while also contributing to the development of new offerings, IP and capability within the firm. If you're a curious and confident consultant with a passion for AI, this is a rare opportunity to help shape how organisations transform with emerging technologies.
  
What Will You Do?
  
As an AI Strategy & Enablement Consultant, your responsibilities will include:

Client delivery: Plan, lead and support AI-related consulting engagements ranging from strategy development and readiness assessments through to design sprints, prototyping and governance frameworks.
Workshop facilitation: Design and run collaborative workshops, to explore opportunities and build client capability.
Strategy and reporting: Develop high-quality deliverables including strategy documents, assessment reports, roadmaps, governance models and executive briefings.
Business development: Contribute to proposals, RFQs and client discussions to shape new opportunities and extend Elysium’s AI practice.
Practice and offering development: Help shape and expand Elysium’s suite of AI service offerings, building tools, frameworks, training content, and accelerators.
Client engagement: Build trusted relationships with client stakeholders, offering clear, pragmatic advice informed by both business context and technology trends.
Cross-functional collaboration: Work closely with colleagues across strategy, design, architecture, data and delivery to bring multidisciplinary perspectives to client problems.
  
What Makes You a Good Fit?
  
We’re looking for someone who:

Has solid experience as a management consultant, enterprise or solution architect, senior business analyst, or similar role in the strategy and advisory space.
Is naturally curious and has a strong interest in artificial intelligence – especially generative AI – and how it can be applied in government and enterprise contexts.
Has strong consulting skills and is able to facilitate workshops, write clearly, structure strategic advice, and communicate with influence.
Can work end-to-end across engagements: shaping opportunities, planning activities, delivering value, and supporting clients through ambiguity.
Is comfortable with both business and technology domains, and able to bridge between them.
Is self-motivated and proactive, excited to build out a new practice area and shape what comes next.
Holds or is eligible for an AGSVA security clearance.
  
You don’t need to be a data scientist or AI engineer, but you should have a solid understanding of how AI works, where it adds value, and the challenges of adopting it responsibly in large organisations.
  
Why Join Elysium Digital?
  
At Elysium Digital, we bring together experienced strategists, designers, technologists and engineers to help our clients solve complex challenges and deliver better digital outcomes. You’ll benefit from:

Meaningful work: Tackle high-impact problems in government and industry, with a focus on emerging technologies and responsible innovation.
Supportive team culture: Be part of a tight-knit Strategy & Advisory practice that values trust, collaboration and professional growth.
Professional development: Access structured pathways to grow your consulting skills and shape your own career trajectory.
Hybrid working model: Flexibility to work from home, client sites, and our Barton office in Canberra. Candidates from other capital cities are welcome.
Creative freedom: Help build a new practice area from the ground up, with room to bring ideas to life and make your mark.

Job Description

About the Role

At SEEK, our Senior Data Scientists play a key role in shaping how millions of people discover meaningful work. As a Senior Data Scientist in the Personalisation team, you’ll lead the design and delivery of end-to-end AI solutions—from defining problems and running experiments to building models and deploying them at scale.

You’ll work on some of SEEK’s most impactful challenges, including developing deep candidate representations, personalising user experiences, and powering adaptive content across our platforms. You’ll collaborate closely with AI Engineers, Product Managers, Designers, and other Data Scientists, while also mentoring and supporting less experienced team members.

This role is perfect for someone who thrives in autonomy, enjoys solving ambiguous problems, and is passionate about creating high-impact, technically excellent solutions. If you’re excited about driving innovation and influencing the direction of AI at SEEK, we’d love to hear from you.

The Team

The Personalisation team sits within SEEK’s Artificial Intelligence & Analytics (AIPS) group and plays a critical role in shaping how candidates interact with our products. We help users find the right opportunities faster by tailoring their experiences using behavioural signals, advanced machine learning, and deep user insights.

As part of a cross-functional, specialised team, we design and deploy models that power intelligent content systems and personalisation engines. We work in quarterly delivery cycles where we explore, prototype, and release solutions that enhance the job discovery experience for millions.

What You’ll Do

In this role, you’ll lead end-to-end personalisation initiatives—working closely with Product Managers, Engineers, and other Data Scientists to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning solutions. You’ll take ownership of the technical direction, shaping the roadmap and driving experimentation to solve complex user experience challenges.

You’ll develop and refine ML models, run rigorous evaluations including A/B tests, and ensure performance, fairness, and reliability in production. You’ll also uncover data-driven insights that influence product strategy, while providing mentorship, technical guidance, and thought leadership across the team.

Qualifications
8+ years’ experience in data science, machine learning, or applied AI, ideally in product-focused environments.
A track record of owning end-to-end ML solutions – from idea and experimentation through to robust, production deployment and iteration.
A strong theoretical foundation in machine learning, statistics, and data analysis, paired with a pragmatic approach to solving real-world problems.
A Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative discipline.
Fluency in Python and SQL, and hands-on experience with modern data science tooling (e.g. Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch).
Practical experience with deep learning, ranking models, recommendation systems, or personalisation frameworks used at scale.
Deep experience in experimentation – designing and running A/B tests, interpreting results, and applying rigorous model evaluation techniques to drive continuous improvement.

Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave

VALD is the world leader in technology for the allied health industry, providing innovative human-measurement technology to over  8,000 clients in over 150 countries. If you have a favorite team in the NBA, EPL, or NFL, there's a good chance they use VALD Technologies.

Since its humble beginnings in 2015 in Brisbane, Australia, VALD has grown to a team of over 300 team members in over 30 countries, with 5 offices across four continents. 

Driven by a multidisciplinary team of researchers, clinicians, sports scientists, designers, developers and engineers, VALD’s suite of systems offer unparalleled insight into human movement, performance, injury risk and rehabilitation.

About the role

VALD is building the world’s largest musculoskeletal database, with over two billion data points and counting. Using this data, we can help practitioners to objectively assess patients’ progress and make more accurate, data-informed treatment decisions. We are looking for an experienced data scientist to join our growing team and build the technical foundations for VALD’s future growth.

In this role, you will: 

Lead improvements in VALD’s normative data generation processes, further enabling practitioners to easily benchmark patients’ performance against a reference group. 

Develop, test and deploy machine learning models for applications such as data quality improvements, rehabilitation trajectory modelling, and predictive device maintenance. 

Apply dimensional modelling expertise to further enhance the Gold layer of the VALD Lakehouse, improving how data products are served to clients. 

Conduct ad-hoc data analysis to support various initiatives across VALD, including patient rehabilitation analytics and customized normative data reporting. 

Contribute to and maintain the data science codebase, implementing best practices in development life cycle management and source control. 

Is this you?

Our ideal candidate has: 

Tertiary qualifications in mathematics, statistics, engineering, or a related field. 
Proficiency in Python (including PySpark and ML libraries), Databricks, SQL, Git, R, Excel, Power BI and R.
Proven experience building machine learning models in Python. 
Expertise in delivering dimensional data models using Kimbal principles. 
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure and its application in data science. 
All the attributes that make a great data scientist – curiosity, attention to detail, teamwork, communication skills, a passion for problem-solving, and a willingness to meet deadlines. 
Ability to handle ambiguity and thrive in a startup environment. 

Why VALD?

An opportunity to be part of revolutionizing the health and performance industries is just the beginning when you join VALD.  

We have a range of benefits we offer to our team, such as:

The opportunity to work in a company that is redefining allied healthcare.
Working with a range of high-performing individuals and teams across a range of disciplines. 
Working with contemporary tooling on industry first applications. 
Casual work attire.
Chefs that prepare you delicious fresh breakfast and lunch.
On-site barista to make your coffee just as you like it.
Team gym, masseuse, and access to physiotherapy and exercise physiology services.

Diversity & Inclusion Commitment

VALD’s best asset is not our technology but our people and culture. A culture of inclusion and diversity is critical to our business. We know diverse teams perform better. It's not a separate initiative – we aim to embed inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We are committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and embracing diversity, including gender, nationality, disability, age, marital/parental status, ethnicity, gender identity, socioeconomic background and sexual orientation. We welcome applications from people from all backgrounds.

Ad Text:
Senior Adviser Finance - Finance Business Partnering, Bauxite, Alumina and Pacific Corporate Operations


Key finance business partner role in a diverse and global organisation:

Advance your career in a dynamic, supportive team environment
Many other additional benefits including flexible working, Rio Tinto share scheme, Rio Discounts Program, contribution to medical costs, leave loading and more!


About the role


Finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs.


Rio Tinto's Weipa operations in Far North QLD, are on the look-out for curious minded individuals who want to take on a rewarding career with us! 2026 is set to be an exciting year for our teams, with activities across a range of site-based initiatives for both people and processes.


We are looking for a Senior Adviser - Finance Business Partnering to be part of a high performing finance team, based in Brisbane with travel to Weipa.

The finance team is committed to providing professional support in understanding, interpreting and communicating business financial performance.

This role will work with multiple stakeholders to provide advice to the operations teams and support the development and execution of strategic decision making.


We are an open, connected global team that includes some of the industry's best and brightest minds. We offer competitive, performance-focused remuneration and a wide range of benefits to reward your contribution. With a global reach, the opportunities to develop and grow your career are broad and exciting.


Reporting to the Manager Finance at Weipa and working in a challenging and exciting environment within the Finance team, you will be:

Responsible for site planning, forecasting and variance reporting.
Interpret monthly, quarterly and annual financial results.
Support Business analysis, including valuation of projects and strategic financial modelling for validation of various business opportunities
Provide support across site and partner with stakeholders to proactively drive and influence financial improvement through analysis and cost leadership deployment
Provide financial training to all levels of the organization to enhance financial acumen
Work with the global finance function teams to implement fit for purpose systems, processes, metrics and reporting
Identify financial opportunities and influence outcomes to enhance the performance of the business
Maintains effective reporting mechanisms to ensure internal stakeholders are provided with insightful and appropriate analysis of financial performance.
Provides analyses to internal stakeholders on all matters of potentially significant financial impact to the company, including economic and competitive trends, pricing, cash flow, product line evaluation and capital additions.
Interprets monthly, quarterly and annual financial results for the assigned perimeter (e.g. Business Unit).


What you'll bring


To be successfully considered for this role we are looking for:

Bachelor and/or professional qualification e.g. CA, CPA, CIMA.
Advanced excel skills
Power BI skills
Management accounting or business analysis background
Strong stakeholder engagement skills
Team orientated approach to delivery of strategic priorities
Proven experience in identifying financial opportunities
Effective presentation skills and ability to present strategic information


It will also be beneficial if you have:

Experience in use of SAP and the Rio Tinto Business Solution (specifically PAM and MMP)
Proficiency with business intelligence programs/techniques (Power BI, EasyMorph and SQL)


This role provides:

A work environment where safety is always the number one priority 
A permanent position working directly for Rio Tinto 
A competitive base salary reflective of your skills and experience with annual incentive program 
Comprehensive medical benefits including subsidised private health insurance for employees and immediate family 
Be recognized for your contribution, your thinking and your hard work, and go home knowing you've helped the world progress.
Attractive share ownership plan 
Company provided insurance cover 
Extensive salary sacrifice & salary packaging options 
Career development & education assistance to further your technical or leadership ambitions 
Ongoing access to family-friendly health and medical wellbeing support 
Leave for all of life's reasons (vacation/annual, paid parental, sick leave, cultural leave)
To better suit different family needs, our paid parental leave approach gives all parents the option to take 18 weeks of paid leave after a new child arrives - at a time that suits them
Exclusive employee discounts including hotel stays, banking, accommodation, cars, retail and more


Where you will be working


The role is based in Brisbane with travel to Rio Tinto Weipa (and working directly at Amrun) also required. Weipa is located 900km north-west of Cairns on the Gulf of Carpentaria and has been operating for over 50 years.

#gts

About Rio Tinto

Rio Tinto is a leading global mining and materials company. We operate in 35 countries where we produce iron ore, copper, aluminium, critical minerals, and other materials needed for the global energy transition and for people, communities, and nations to thrive.

We have been mining for 150 years and operate with knowledge built up across generations and continents. Our purpose is finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs - striving for innovation and continuous improvement to produce materials with low emissions and to the right environmental, social and governance standards. But we can't do it on our own, so we're focused on creating partnerships to solve problems, create win-win situations and meet opportunities.

Respect and Inclusion

At Rio Tinto, we particularly welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, the LGBTI+ community, mature workers, people with disabilities and people from different cultural backgrounds.

We are committed to an inclusive environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We want our people to feel that all voices are heard, all cultures respected and that a variety of perspectives are not only welcome - they are essential to our success. We treat each other fairly and with dignity regardless of race, gender, nationality, ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation or anything else that makes us different.

Job Title: IT Project Manager with strong Business Analysis
Ad Text:
IT Project Manager with strong Business Analysis -Sydney/Anywhere in Australia

We are seeking a hands-on IT Project Manager with strong Business Analysis capability to support the early stages of several IT initiatives.

This role will focus on understanding current systems and processes, defining requirements, and preparing projects to move through governance and into delivery.

The role requires someone who is comfortable wearing both Project Manager and Business Analyst hats and working closely with business and technical stakeholders.

Senior Business Analysts who are looking to transition into Project Management are strongly encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities

Engage with stakeholders to understand current systems, processes, and operational challenges

Document current state processes, systems, and dependencies

Facilitate discussions to define business requirements and improvement opportunities

Prepare future state recommendations and solution options

Develop key project artefacts including:

Project briefs

Business cases

Project plans

Requirements documentation

Governance submissions

Coordinate activities required to move projects through internal governance and approval processes

Support planning and prioritisation of initiatives aligned to the organisation’s IT roadmap

Work closely with internal teams to prepare projects for delivery readiness

Key Skills and Experience

8+ years experience in IT project delivery and/or business analysis

3+ years of experience in IT Project Management

Demonstrated experience supporting IT systems or digital transformation initiatives

Strong experience preparing project initiation artefacts and governance documentation

Ability to translate business needs into structured requirements and delivery plans

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills

Ability to work independently in early-stage projects where scope may evolve

Ideal Candidate Profile

Background as Senior Business Analyst, Project Manager, or hybrid PM/BA

Experience working across systems delivery and process improvement initiatives

Comfortable working in hands-on roles in smaller or early-stage projects

Senior Business Analysts seeking to move into Project Management will be highly regarded

#ITProjectManager #SeniorBusinessAnalyst #PMBA #ProjectDelivery #BusinessAnalysis #ProjectGovernance #StakeholderManagement #RequirementsGathering #ProcessImprovement #SystemsDelivery #ITTransformation #ProjectPlanning #DigitalTransformation #ITRoadmap #BusinessProcessMapping #AgileDelivery #DeliveryReadiness #BAtoPM

Senior Workday Business Analyst | HCM & Recruitment Lead

Location: Sydney (Hybrid - 3 days per week on-site)

Program: Large-Scale Workday Transformation

The Mission

We are looking for a powerhouse Senior Workday Business Analyst to join a flagship transformation program for one of Sydney’s leading tertiary institutions. This isn’t just a "business as usual" role—you will be the bridge between our internal stakeholders and our delivery partner, ensuring that the Core HCM, Recruitment, Absence, and Compensation modules are not just "implemented," but optimized for a complex, academic environment.

With a heavy emphasis on the Recruitment module, you will set the benchmark for documentation, process mapping, and functional excellence.

Your Impact

Module Ownership: Lead the functional analysis for Workday Recruitment, Core HCM, Absence, and Compensation.

Vendor Management: Partner closely with our implementation partner to ensure delivery hits the mark, challenging assumptions and maintaining high documentation standards.

Leadership: Provide mentorship and potentially direct supervision to a small team of BAs, ensuring a unified approach to requirements gathering.

Stakeholder Navigation: Translate complex Workday functionality into business value for academic and professional staff who may be new to the platform.

Process Excellence: Audit and improve the current state of documentation (BRDs, FDS, Process Maps) to ensure a seamless transition to the "To-Be" state.

The Technical Toolkit

The Non-Negotiables: You have a proven track record of delivering multiple end-to-end Workday implementations.

Functional Depth: Expert-level knowledge of Workday Recruitment is essential, complemented by strong proficiency in Core HCM, Absence, and Compensation.

Communication Mastery: You possess the "soft skills" required to manage diverse stakeholders in a complex, multi-layered environment like Higher Education.

Documentation Rigour: You take pride in creating clear, concise, and scalable functional specifications.

The "Cherry on Top" (Preferred)

Advanced Comp & Performance: Experience in Advanced Compensation and Performance Management modules.

Sector Experience: Prior experience within the Australian Higher Education sector (understanding the nuances of academic cycles and enterprise agreements).

Why Join This Program?

This is a high-visibility role within a major Sydney institution. You’ll have the autonomy to shape a small team, the challenge of a large-scale implementation, and the reward of seeing your work transform the employee experience for thousands of staff.

Job Title: Senior Pricing Actuary Advisor - Earnix (12 month contract + extensions)
Ad Text:
We are seeking a talented and experienced Senior Pricing Actuary Advisor with extensive Earnix experience to join our dynamic team and contribute to our major modernization program.
Key Responsibilities:

Pricing Algorithm Development: Utilize your extensive knowledge in general insurance pricing to develop and optimize pricing models, starting with home and motor algorithms.
Earnix Expertise: Leverage your experience with Earnix to support the transition and enhancement of our pricing systems.
Modernization Program: Contribute to a large-scale modernization program aimed at uplifting and delivering advanced pricing capabilities.
Collaboration with Consultants: Work closely with Earnix consultants to build and implement pricing solutions.
Operational Support: Support the team in executing tasks and ensuring seamless integration of pricing models into Earnix.
Key Requirements:

Experience in Pricing: Proven track record in general insurance pricing, particularly in home and motor insurance.
Earnix Proficiency: Demonstrated experience with Earnix is highly regulated and essential.
Technical Skills: Familiarity with pricing model development and optimization.
Team Collaboration: Ability to work effectively within a team, contributing to collective success.
Experience Level: 3-5+ years of relevant experience in pricing algorithm development and Earnix.

If you are passionate about pricing algorithms and have significant experience with Earnix, we would love to hear from you. Click apply or email kiara@correlateresources.com

Job Title: Senior Governance, Risk and Compliance Analyst
Ad Text:
Senior Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Analyst
Location: CBD, Sydney
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role An exciting opportunity exists for an experienced Senior Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Analyst to join a high-performing technology-focused organisation. This role plays a critical part in strengthening governance, risk management, and compliance capabilities across the organisation.

Working closely with senior leadership, you will be responsible for driving governance and risk initiatives, supporting security and compliance frameworks, and ensuring alignment with industry standards and regulatory requirements. You will collaborate with technology, engineering, product, and business teams to design and implement effective controls, policies, and risk management practices.

Key Responsibilities
Provide subject matter expertise and guidance on governance, risk, and compliance matters across the organisation.
Support the technology strategy and roadmap by ensuring governance and compliance requirements are embedded in new initiatives and services.
Assist with the implementation and certification of ISO 42001:2023.
Maintain and enhance the Information Security Management System (ISMS) to support compliance with ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II.
Review and improve technology policies, standards, and procedures to address emerging security threats and evolving regulatory requirements.
Support the organisation’s risk management program, identifying potential risks across systems, applications, and infrastructure.
Coordinate risk mitigation activities in partnership with engineering, product, technology, and business teams.
Conduct vendor due diligence and third-party risk assessments.
Support internal and external audit processes and preparation activities.
Contribute to business impact analysis and maintain the enterprise risk register.
Perform compliance gap assessments and monitor developments in regulatory and cybersecurity landscapes.
Measures of Success
Ongoing review and improvement of ISMS policies, procedures, and governance documentation.
Effective implementation and maintenance of the organisation’s Risk Management Framework.
Timely completion of vendor due diligence and third-party risk assessments.
Successful preparation and participation in ISO27001, ISO42001, and SOC2 audits.
Contribution to the implementation of emerging AI governance and compliance standards.
Skills & Experience
Demonstrated experience in a Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) role within a technology or cybersecurity environment.
Experience implementing and maintaining security and compliance frameworks.
Strong knowledge of cybersecurity governance frameworks, regulations, and standards.
Solid understanding of cybersecurity principles, technologies, and risk management practices.
Experience managing risk registers, risk tooling, and reporting processes.
Strong experience supporting audit and certification processes.
Proven ability to lead organisational change initiatives related to compliance and governance.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work independently while collaborating across multiple teams.
Frameworks & Standards Experience Experience working with several of the following frameworks is highly regarded:

ISO 27001
ISO 42001
SOC 2
NIST
CIS Controls
Essential Eight
GDPR
DORA
AI governance and security standards
Certifications (Desirable)
CISM
CISA
CISSP
ISO 27001 Lead Implementer or Lead Auditor
Why Apply?
This role offers the opportunity to work in a modern technology environment where governance, security, and compliance play a strategic role in business operations. You will collaborate with experienced professionals and contribute to strengthening enterprise risk and compliance capabilities in a growing organisation.
